title: Mammillaria johnstonii
taxonomy: Mammillaria johnstonii is a species of cactus in the family Cactaceae. It belongs to the subfamily Cactoideae and tribe Cacteae. Within the genus Mammillaria, Mammillaria johnstonii is classified as a member of the subgenus Mammillaria. The classification further breaks down into the series Johnstoniae, where Mammillaria johnstonii is a part of. This specific classification helps to group Mammillaria johnstonii with other similar species within the plant kingdom.
uses: Mammillaria johnstonii is a species of cactus known for its potential medicinal uses. Traditionally, it has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions. The plant's extracts are said to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may contribute to its therapeutic effects. Furthermore, Mammillaria johnstonii is believed to have antiseptic and antibacterial properties, making it a potential remedy for wounds and infections. However, more research is needed to fully understand the plant's medicinal properties and potential applications.

preparations: Mammillaria johnstonii is not a commonly used plant in herbal preparations, and there is limited information available on its traditional or modern uses in medicine. intro: Mammillaria johnstonii, also known as Johnston's Pincushion Cactus, is a small, globular cactus species native to Mexico, characterized by its dense, fine spines and funnel-shaped flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever reduction, sore throats, and digestive problems, thanks to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Mammillaria johnstonii include flavonoids, alkaloids, and glycosides, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant are infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used to create teas, syrups, and medicinal tonics.
